content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 18, 1991


Marsha Loveridge
4590 University Ave.
Riverside, CA 92501

Re: Your Request For Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-90-706

Dear Ms. Loveridge:

You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Because you
have not indicated that you are seeking advice on behalf of a
person who has duties under the Act, we are treating your letter
as a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
18329(c)(4) (copy enclosed.)2/

QUESTION

What is the proper procedure for reporting a gift that is
given away or returned?

CONCLUSION

With the exception of passes or season tickets for admission
to or access to facilities such as theaters or amusement parks, an
official must disclose receipt of a gift unless the donor is
reimbursed for the gift or the gift is returned, unused, to the

___________________________
1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of
Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Ms. M. Loveridge
January 18, 1991
Page 2


donor, or is donated, unused, to a charitable organization or a
government agency without claiming any deduction for tax purposes.
The reimbursement, return or donation must occur within 30 days of
receipt.  A gift which is not reimbursed and which is retained by
the official is deemed to have been accepted and must be disclosed
even if the official disclaims or does not use the gift or gives
the gift to another person.

     A pass or season ticket for admission to or access to
facilities (such as passes to theaters) has no value if the
official receiving the pass or season ticket does not use it and
does not give the pass or season ticket to another person.

                              DISCUSSION

     Regulation 18726.1(a) (copy enclosed) states that a gift is
provided to, received by, or promised to an official at the time
the offer is extended if the gift is offered to the official in
such a manner that it is within the official's discretion whether
or not to accept it and the official ultimately decides to accept
the gift.  A gift is deemed accepted if it is tendered and comes
under the control of the official and the official and does not
return it, donate it, or reimburse the donor for it as provided
below.  Discarding or otherwise turning a gift over to another
person constitutes acceptance of the gift by the official.

     Regulation 18726.1(b) provides that an official has not
received a gift if the official returns, donates, or reimburses
the donor in the following manner:

     (1) The official returns the gift, unused, within 30 days of
receipt, to the donor or his or her agent or intermediary.

     (2)  The official donates the gift, unused, within 30 days of
receipt, to a charitable organization without claiming it as
a charitable contribution for tax purposes.

     (3)  The official donates the gift, unused, within 30 days of
receipt, to a state, local or federal government agency
without claiming any deduction for tax purposes.

     (4) The official reimburses the donor or his or her agent or
intermediary for the gift within 30 days of receipt.

     Per your request, enclosed is a copy of Regulation 18726.3,
which provides that for purposes of disclosure, the value of a
pass or season ticket for admission to or access to facilities is

Ms. M Loveridge
January 18, 1991
Page 3

the fair market value of the actual use by the official, including
any guests accompanying the official, plus the fair market value
of any possible use by any person or persons to whom the official
transfers the privilege or use of the pass or season ticket.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

by: Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division


Enclosures
